markdown:
<title level="1" right="General Skill Action " pfs="Standard">[Earn Income](/Skills.aspx?ID=21&General=true) (Trained)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 228

You can use a skill to earn money during downtime. You must be trained in the skill to do so. This takes time to set up, and your income depends on your proficiency rank and how lucrative a task you can find. Because this process requires a significant amount of time and involves tracking things outside the progress of adventures, it won't come up in every campaign.

In some cases, the GM might let you use a different skill to Earn Income through specialized work. Usually, this is scholarly work, such as using [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) in a monastery to study old texts—but giving sermons at a church would still fall under [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) instead of Religion. You also might be able to use physical skills to make money, such as using [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) to perform feats in a circus or Thievery to pick pockets. If you're using a skill other than [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), or Performance, the DC tends to be significantly higher. 

[Earn Income Calculator](/earn-income-calculator)

<title level="2">Skill Uses</title>
[[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)] Earn Income by crafting goods for the market.

[[Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)] Earn Income by using your knowledge to practice a trade.

[[Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45)] Earn Income by staging a performance.

<title level="2" right="">Income Examples</title>The following examples show the kinds of tasks your character might take on to Earn Income during low-level and high-level play. 

<title level="3" right="">Harsk Makes Tea</title> Harsk is a 3rd-level ranger and an expert at harvesting and brewing tea. He has a Tea Lore modifier of +7. He has 30 days of downtime at his disposal and decides to work at a prestigious local tea house. The GM decides this is a 5th-level task if Harsk wants to assist the tea master, or a 2nd-level task if he wants to serve tea. Harsk chooses the tougher task, and the GM secretly sets the DC at 20.

 Harsk rolls a 4 on his Tea Lore check for a result of 11. Poor Harsk has failed! He earns only 2 sp for his efforts and continues working for 3 more days, for a total of 8 sp.

 At that point, the GM offers Harsk a choice: either he can finish out the week with the tea master and look for a new job, or he can lower his ambitions and serve in the tea house. Harsk, now more aware of his own capabilities, accepts the less prestigious job for now. He moves to his new job and attempts a new Tea Lore check against DC 16. Rolling a 19, he gets a result of 26—a critical success! He earns 5 sp per day (like a success at a 3rd-level task). The GM rules that demand will be high enough that Harsk can work there for the remainder of his downtime if he so chooses, a total of 26 days. Harsk accepts and earns a total of 138 sp (13 gp, 8 sp) that month. 

<title level="3" right="">Lem Performs</title> Lem is a 16th-level bard and legendary with his flute. He has a Performance modifier of +31 with his enchanted flute. With 30 days of downtime ahead of him, Lem wonders if he can find something that might excite him more than performing in front of a bunch of stuffy nobles. He finds a momentous offer indeed—a performance in a celestial realm, and Lem's patron goddess Shelyn might even be in attendance! This is a 20th-level task, and the GM secretly sets the DC at 40.

 Lem rolls an 11 on his Performance check for a result of 42. Success! The engagement lasts for a week, and at the end, the grateful celestials present Lem with a beautiful living diamond rose in constant bloom worth 1,400 gold pieces (200 gp per day for 7 days).

 With 23 days of downtime left, Lem accepts a 14th-level task performing at a prestigious bardic college for members of a royal court. The GM secretly sets the DC at 32, and Lem critically succeeds, earning 28 gp per day for a total of 644 gp. Between the two performances, Lem has earned just over 2,000 gold pieces during his downtime—though he's not sure he'll ever sell that rose.

<title level="2" right="">Extra Preparation</title> When Earning Income, you might be able to spend days of downtime to prepare for your task, which adjusts the DC of the skill check. This might involve rehearsing a play, studying a topic, and so on. The GM determines how long preparation takes and how much the DC changes. This is most useful when you're trying a task that's higher level than you; otherwise such tasks have an increased DC! 

<title level="2" right="">Ending Or Interrupting Tasks</title> When a task you're doing is complete, or if you stop in the middle of one, you normally have to find a new task if you want to keep Earning Income. For instance, if you quit your job working at the docks, you'll need to find another place of employment instead of picking up where you left off. This usually takes 1 day or more of downtime looking for leads on new jobs.

 However, you might pause a task due to an adventure or event that wouldn't prevent you from returning to the old job later. The GM might decide that you can pick up where you left off, assuming the task hasn't been completed by others in your absence. Whether you roll a new skill check when you resume is also up to the GM. Generally speaking, if you had a good initial roll and want to keep it, you can, but if you had a bad initial roll, you can't try for a better one by pausing to do something else. If your statistics changed during the break—usually because you leveled up while adventuring—you can attempt a new check.

<document level="2" id="action-2364" />

<title level="2">Crafting Goods for the Market [[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)]</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 229

Using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), you can work at producing common items for the market. It's usually easy to find work making basic items whose level is 1 or 2 below your settlement's level.

 Higher-level tasks represent special commissions, which might require you to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) a specific item using the Craft downtime activity and sell it to a buyer at full Price. These opportunities don't occur as often and might have special requirements—or serious consequences if you disappoint a prominent client.

<title level="2">Practicing a Trade [[Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)]</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 230

You apply the practical benefits of one of your [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) specialties during downtime by practicing your trade. This is most effective for Lore specialties such as business, law, or sailing, where there's high demand for workers. The GM might increase the DC or determine only low-level tasks are available if you're attempting to use an obscure Lore skill to Earn Income. You might also need specialized tools to accept a job, like mining tools to work in a mine or a merchant's scale to buy and sell valuables in a market.

<title level="2">Staging a Performance [[Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45)]</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 230

You perform for an audience to make money. The available audiences determine the level of your task, since more discerning audiences are harder to impress but provide a bigger payout. The GM determines the task level based on the audiences available. Performing for a typical audience of commoners on the street is a level 0 task, but a performance for a group of artisans with more refined tastes might be a 2nd- or 3rd-level task, and ones for merchants, nobility, and royalty are increasingly higher level.

 Your degree of success determines whether you moved your audience and whether you were rewarded with applause or rotten fruit.
name: Earn Income

<additional-info>
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 228
</additional-info>

<title level="1" right="General Skill Action " pfs="Standard">[Identify Magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=22&General=true) (Trained)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 230

Using the skill related to the appropriate tradition, as explained in the Magical Traditions and Skills sidebar, you can attempt to identify a magical item, location, or ongoing effect. In many cases, you can use a skill to attempt to Identify Magic of a tradition other than your own at a higher DC. The GM determines whether you can do this and what the DC is.

<title level="2">Skill Uses</title>
[[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)] Identify Magic , particularly magic of the [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) tradition.

[[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)] Identify Magic , particularly magic of the [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) tradition.

[[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)] Identify Magic , particularly magic of the [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) tradition.

[[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)] Identify Magic , particularly magic of the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) tradition.

<document level="2" id="action-2365" />
name: Identify Magic

<title level="1" right="General Skill Action " pfs="Standard">[Learn a Spell](/Skills.aspx?ID=23&General=true) (Trained)</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 230

If you're a spellcaster, you can use the skill corresponding to your magical tradition to learn a new spell of that tradition. The Learning a Spell table lists the Price of the materials needed to Learn a Spell of each rank. Learning a Spell is most useful for classes that use a limited list of spells, like the [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=3), [witch](/Classes.aspx?ID=16), or [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12), though other classes might use it to gain access to [rare](/Traits.aspx?ID=137) or [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) spells.

<title level="2">Skill Uses</title>
[[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)] Learn a Spell from the [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) tradition.

[[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)] Learn a Spell from the [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) tradition.

[[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)] Learn a Spell from the [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) tradition.

[[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)] Learn a Spell from the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) tradition.

<document level="2" id="action-2366" />
name: Learn a Spell

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 231 To remember useful information on a topic, you can attempt to Recall Knowledge. This action is one you're likely to use frequently. Learning more about the world and people around you is one of the best ways to inform your decisions, and Recalling Knowledge can help you figure out how to best fight monsters and figure out puzzles and social challenges. You might know basic information about something without needing to attempt a check, but Recall Knowledge requires you to stop and think for a moment so you can recollect more specific facts and apply them. You might even need to spend time investigating first. For instance, to use [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) to learn the cause of death, you might need to conduct a forensic examination before attempting to Recall Knowledge. Skill Uses [[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)] Recall Knowledge about arcane theories; magic traditions; creatures of arcane significance (like [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547), and [elementals](/Traits.aspx?ID=587)); and the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), Elemental Planes, and [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36). [[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)] Recall Knowledge about alchemical reactions, the value of items, engineering, unusual materials, and alchemical or mechanical creatures. The GM determines which creatures this applies to, but it usually includes [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35). [[Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)] Recall Knowledge about the subject of the Lore skill's subcategory. [[Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)] Recall Knowledge about diseases, injuries, poisons, and other ailments. You can use this to perform forensic examinations if you spend 10 minutes (or more, as determined by the GM) checking for evidence such as wound patterns. This is most useful when determining how a body was injured or killed. [[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)] Recall Knowledge about fauna, flora, geography, weather, the environment, creatures of natural origin (like [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668)), the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), and the Elemental Planes. [[Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)] Recall Knowledge about ancient mysteries; obscure philosophies; creatures of occult significance (like [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=149), and [oozes](/Traits.aspx?ID=665)); and esoteric planes like the [Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), [Creation's Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27), the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9), [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), and [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28). [[Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)] Recall Knowledge about divine agents, the finer points of theology, obscure myths regarding a faith, and creatures of religious significance (like [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160)), [Creation's Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27), [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28), and the Outer Sphere. [[Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)] Recall Knowledge about local history, important personalities, legal institutions, societal structure, and humanoid cultures. The GM might allow Society to apply to other creatures that are major elements of society in your region, such as the draconic nobility in a kingdom of humans ruled by dragons. Item Bonuses for Recall Knowledge
Item Bonus Level Category Consumable Note
[Mnemonic Feather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3523) +1 3 [Held Items](/equipment/held-items) No Attempts to Recall Knowledge about a topic contained within the book that the feather is placed in
[Hoax-Hunter's Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4115) +1 3 [Adventuring Gear](/equipment/adventuring-gear) No Recall Knowledge about a creature you are studying
[Echo Token](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3521) +1 1 [Other Consumables](/equipment/consumables/other-consumables) Yes A specific type of Lore
[Krasovnatype](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3566) +1 4 [Gadgets](/equipment/consumables/gadgets) Yes Recall Knowledge check regarding the creature imprinted.
[Memory Ribbon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3768) +2 4 [Other Consumables](/equipment/consumables/other-consumables) Yes Item bonus to Recall Knowledge
name: Recall Knowledge

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 232 If you need to provide food and shelter, you can use the Subsist downtime activity. This typically uses [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) if you're in a settlement or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) if you're in the wild. Skill Uses [[Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)] Subsist in a settlement by finding shelter, scrounging, or begging for food. Item Bonus Level Category Consumable Note
[Emperor's Peak Quartz Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3766) +1 3 [Other Worn Items](/equipment/worn-items/other-worn-items) No Item bonus to Survival checks to Subsist. +3 if in mountainous terrain

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[Abjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 207

As an abjurer, you master the art of protection, strengthening defenses, preventing attacks, and even turning magic against itself. You understand that an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure. You add one 1st-level abjuration spell (such as [_feather fall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=111)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_protective ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=534) school spell.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 207

As a conjurer, you summon creatures and objects from places beyond, and use magic to transport to distant locales. You understand that the true key to victory is strength in numbers. You add one 1st-level conjuration spell (such as [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_augment summoning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=521) school spell.

You add one 1st-level divination spell (such as [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=345)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_diviner’s sight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=525) school spell.

You add one 1st-level enchantment spell (such as [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_charming words_](/Spells.aspx?ID=523) school spell.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 208

As an evoker, you revel in the raw power of magic, using it to create and destroy with ease. You can call forth elements, forces, and energy to devastate your foes or to assist you in other ways. You understand that the most direct approach is the most elegant. You add one 1st-level evocation spell (such as [_shocking grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_force bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=529) school spell.

As a necromancer, you call upon the powers of life and death. While your school is often vilified for its association with raising the undead, you understand that control over life also means control over healing. You add one 1st-level necromancy spell (such as [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=141)) to your spellbook. You learn the [_call of the grave_](/Spells.aspx?ID=522) school spell.

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[Universalist](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 210

Instead of specializing narrowly in an arcane school, you can become a universalist wizard—by studying all the schools equally, you devote yourself to understanding the full breadth of the arcane arts. For each level of wizard spell slots you have, you can use [Drain Bonded Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=13) once per day to recall a spell of that level (instead of using it only once per day in total). You gain an extra wizard class feat, and you add one 1st-level spell of your choice to your spellbook.
name: Universalist

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Ars Grammatica](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=16)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 198

Runes and wards, numbers and letters—they underpin all magic, making them the logical subject for a wizard who studies fundamental forces. Perhaps you studied at the Pathfinder Society's School of Spells or a similar institution, but whether you're lacing your words with magic to compel others, casting wards around your workshop, or destabilizing the very structure of an opponent's spells, you know this unassuming school carries elegant power.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673)
- 1st:
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Disguise Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491), [Runic Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658)
- 2nd:
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723)
- 3rd:
[Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
- 4th:
[Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
- 5th:
[Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)
- 6th:
[Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650), [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=1683)
- 7th:
[Contingency](/Spells.aspx?ID=1472), [Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635)
- 8th:
[Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- 9th:
[Detonate Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Protective Wards](/Spells.aspx?ID=1894)
- Advanced: [Rune of Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1895)
name: School of Ars Grammatica

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of the Boundary](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 199

Why use your magic to affect something as pedestrian as the physical world? Whether you studied at the College of Dimensional Studies in Katapesh or an underground school in haunted Ustalav, you've turned your magic past the Universe to the forces beyond, summoning spirits and shades, manipulating dimensions and planes, and treading in a place not meant for mortals.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- 1st:

- 2nd:
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
- 3rd:
[Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449), [Ghostly Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1544)
- 4th:
[Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- 5th:
[Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578)
- 6th:
[Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735)
- 7th:
[Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576)
- 8th:
[Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- 9th:
[Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Fortify Summoning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1898)
- Advanced: [Spiral of Horrors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1899)
name: School of the Boundary

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Civic Wizardry](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=18)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 199

Whether you studied in Manaket's Occularium or the Academy of Applied Magic, you learned that the fruits of arcane studies—like any other field—should ultimately help the common citizen. You've learned the humble art of construction, of finding lost people and things, of moving speedily among buildings and moats—yet these same arts can be turned to demolition, and the constructs you animate to build bridges can just as easily tear them down.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- 1st:
[Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), [Pummeling Rubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1642), [Summon Construct](/Spells.aspx?ID=1696)
- 2nd:
[Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756)
- 3rd:
[Cozy Cabin](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659)
- 4th:
[Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732)
- 5th:
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- 6th:
[Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749)
- 7th:
[Planar Palace](/Spells.aspx?ID=1634), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652)
- 8th:
[Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507), [Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633)
- 9th:
[Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Earthworks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1900)
- Advanced: [Community Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=1901)
name: School of Civic Wizardry

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 200

As a scholar, you know all too well the importance of a sound mind. Thus, you attended a school—like the Farseer Tower or the Stone of the Seers—that taught the arts of befuddling lesser minds with figments and illusions or implanted sensations and memories.

**Curriculum**

- 3rd:

- 6th:

- 7th:
[Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754)
- 8th:
[Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1730)
- 9th:
[Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Charming Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1902)
- Advanced: [Invisibility Cloak](/Spells.aspx?ID=1903)

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Protean Form](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=20)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 200

The uninitiated often think of wizards as cerebral, focused on their studies more than the body, yet your school of magic taught of the relationship between the two. Your magic, whether learned at a storied institution like Kintargo's Alabaster Academy or someplace more sinister, like the Fleshforges of Nex, focuses on the ways that living matter can be convinced into another shape for a time, allowing you to polymorph a seed into a vine, a human into a beast, or a harmless germ into a deadly toxin.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- 1st:
[Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580), [Pest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626), [Spider Sting](/Spells.aspx?ID=1684)
- 2nd:
[Enlarge](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514), [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560)
- 3rd:
[Feet to Fins](/Spells.aspx?ID=1525), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736)
- 4th:
[Mountain Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738)
- 5th:
[Elemental Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1510), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722)
- 6th:
[Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [Petrify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628)
- 7th:
[Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1505), [Fiery Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1527)
- 8th:
[Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [Monstrosity Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1608)
- 9th:
[Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1599)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Scramble Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1904)
- Advanced: [Shifting Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1905)
name: School of Protean Form

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 200 You eschew the idea that magic can be neatly expressed by the teachings of any single school or college, instead directing your self-study to pick up the best of every school of magic. In doing so, you'll find the truths that lie at the intersection of each school, coming closer to the ideal nature of arcane magic. One day, you'll uncover that single elegant theory detailing all magic (perhaps a theory bearing your name?), but until then, your studies continue. **No Curriculum** You don't have a set curriculum, and so you don't have curriculum spells and can't benefit from abilities that specifically affect them. Instead, you gain an additional 1st-level wizard class feat, and you add one 1st- rank spell of your choice to your spellbook to represent your diverse studies.
Your studies into the very nature of magic itself have let you use it more efficiently—instead of using Drain Bonded Item only once per day, you can use it once per day for each rank of spell you can cast, recalling a spell of that rank each time. **School Spells** - Initial: [Hand of the Apprentice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1906) - Advanced: [Interdisciplinary Incantation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1907)
name: School of Unified Magical Theory

<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Battle Magic](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=22)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 199

Magic is power, and there are always those who will use power for the art of battle. You may have studied in the military of a nation of the brink of war—Cheliax and Andoran perhaps, or Geb and Nex. You summon whirling energies that can lay waste to both soldiers and armies, while being sure not to neglect arcane countermeasures for common tactical complications or the shields and defenses that keep combatants alive on the battlefield.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- 1st:
[Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Mystic Armor](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611)
- 2nd:
[Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651)
- 3rd:
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530)
- 4th:
[Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758)
- 5th:
[Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [Impaling Spike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571)
- 6th:
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492)
- 7th:
[Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512), [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726)
- 8th:
[Arctic Rift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1444), [Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484)
- 9th:
[Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Force Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1896)
- Advanced: [Energy Absorption](/Spells.aspx?ID=1897)
name: School of Battle Magic

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 99

While any thug can commit a murder, proper assassination requires finesse and guile. Deep within the Crimson Citadel, the Red Mantis assassins have developed a specific selection of spells they teach to all magically capable members. These spells complement the assassins’ clandestine and deadly methods.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546)
- 1st:

- 2nd:
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606)
- 3rd:
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)
- 4th:
[Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- 5th:
[Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570)
- 6th:
[Mislead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630)
- 7th:
[Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1505), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640)
- 8th:
[Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- 9th:
[Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Debilitating Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=2130)
- Advanced: [Shroud of the Mantis](/Spells.aspx?ID=2131)

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Thassilonian Rune Magic](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=25)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 114

This curriculum, taught at a few select academies in New Thassilon, can be learned only by wizards with the [runelord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=303) class archetype. You have begun to study one of the seven runes of sin, granting you great power but at the cost of anathematic magic. All runelords gain the below curriculum and school spells. In addition, you must choose one of the seven sins to specialize in. You add your sin’s spells and initial school spells to your curriculum. The addictive nature of sin magic prevents you from using magic in ways aligned with other sins, represented by that sin’s anathema. Casting a sin spell never invokes the anathema of its school of sin.

 Unlike other wizards, some of your curriculum spells may be spells that don’t typically appear on the arcane spell list (marked with an asterisk). You still cast these spells as arcane spells, the unifying knowledge of sin bridging the gap between disparate magics.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673)
- 1st:
[Mystic Armor](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [Runic Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657), [Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
- 2nd:
[Darkvision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
- 3rd:
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
- 4th:
[Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)
- 5th:
[Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665)
- 6th:
[Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
- 7th:
[Contingency](/Spells.aspx?ID=1472), [Spell Riposte](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027)
- 8th:
[Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- 9th:
[Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537)

**School Spells**

- Advanced: [Personal Runewell](/Spells.aspx?ID=2325)
name: School of Thassilonian Rune Magic

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of the Reclamation](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=26)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 32

Whether it's been lost to war, abandoned during calamity, or simply buried by the ages, there is as much important knowledge to be found in the hidden corners of the world as in the schools and libraries of the academy. Your study of magic in the service of rediscovering lost knowledge for the Sarkoris Reclamation has taught you that much that was once lost can still be found, but only if someone is willing to seek it out and retrieve it. You've focused your magic on reaching the world's hidden and forgotten places, whether they are found in a distant ruin or the depths of a lost memory, and bringing their knowledge back to the surface—whatever the cost.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- 1st:
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597)
- 2nd:
[Knock](/Spells.aspx?ID=1581), [Mental Map](/Spells.aspx?ID=2295)
- 3rd:
[Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588), [Overwhelming Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=2296)
- 4th:
[Bridge of Vines](/Spells.aspx?ID=2294), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667)
- 5th:
[Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Scouting Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661)
- 6th:
[Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749)
- 7th:
[Restore Ground](/Spells.aspx?ID=2297), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652)
- 8th:
[Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734)
- 9th:
[Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1599)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Grasping Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=2292)
- Advanced: [Unsettling Knowledge](/Spells.aspx?ID=2293)
name: School of the Reclamation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Kalistrade](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=27)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 75

Power and money are closely intertwined, and you recognize the potential for magic to assist in your pursuit of wealth and influence. Though you might study this school without joining the [Prophets of Kalistrade](/Deities.aspx?ID=23), most who do so are either a part of the organization or local to Kerse and therefore used to the customs and culture of the Kalistocrats. You deploy magic to help you see through the illusions and trickery of others. At the same time, you push boundaries and exploit loopholes to get ahead of your competitors.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- 1st:
[Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579)
- 2nd:
[Ghostly Carrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=1543), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577)
- 3rd:
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
- 4th:
[Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
- 5th:
[Imaginary Lockbox](/Spells.aspx?ID=2002), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692)
- 6th:
[Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662)
- 7th:
[Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652)
- 8th:
[Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490), [Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556)
- 9th:
[Resplendent Mansion](/Spells.aspx?ID=2018)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Unexpected Windfall](/Spells.aspx?ID=2465)
- Advanced: [Capital Dividend](/Spells.aspx?ID=2466)
name: School of Kalistrade

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Standard">[School of Gates](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=28)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 148

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option._

Some are content to stay in place and let the world shape them, but you prefer to move at your own pace. You’ve studied the ancient aiudara and other structures to learn the magic of transposition, using your knowledge to move across great distances and even planes. With knowledge as your path and magic as your key, there’s no door you can’t open, and no world you won’t explore.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- 1st:
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=1589), [Thoughtful Gift](/Spells.aspx?ID=2037)
- 2nd:
[Knock](/Spells.aspx?ID=1581), [Warping Pull](/Spells.aspx?ID=2486)
- 3rd:
[Echo Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=2481), [Trade Items](/Spells.aspx?ID=2485)
- 4th:
[Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- 5th:
[King's Castle](/Spells.aspx?ID=2483), [Magic Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1591)
- 6th:
[Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1978), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720)
- 7th:
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635)
- 8th:
[Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Sudden Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=2484)
- 9th:
[Forest of Gates](/Spells.aspx?ID=2482)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Friendly Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=2479)
- Advanced: [Rapid Retreat](/Spells.aspx?ID=2480)
name: School of Gates

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Wizard Arcane School " pfs="Limited">[School of Magical Technologies](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=29)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 81

At the height of ancient Azlant, the study of magical technologies—better known as magitech—was considered among the most prestigious of arcane specializations, and practitioners were both well regarded and well paid. While some magitech specialists trained in academies or as apprentices, others studied at religious cloisters dedicated to the worship of [Amaznen](/Deities.aspx?ID=404), a now-dead god of invention and [magic](/Domains.aspx?ID=82). Today, practitioners are more likely to learn from scraps of ancient spellbooks, forgotten lore unearthed in Azlanti ruins, or from a time‑displaced graduate like Anoreth Zorillen. Modern magitech schools exist today, divorced from their ancient Azlanti origins but no less prestigious, including the famed Clockwork Cathedral in Absalom and temples of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), goddess of clockwork and invention.

As a student of magical technologies, you’ve learned the art of fusing magic with mundane technology to create something truly unique.

**Curriculum**
- Cantrips:
[Needle Darts](/Spells.aspx?ID=1375), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
- 1st:
[Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597), [Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658), [Summon Construct](/Spells.aspx?ID=1696)
- 2nd:
[Animated Assault](/Spells.aspx?ID=1964), [Dismantle](/Spells.aspx?ID=1987)
- 3rd:
[Shrink Item](/Spells.aspx?ID=2026), [Wooden Double](/Spells.aspx?ID=1416)
- 4th:
[Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758)
- 5th:
[Impaling Spike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- 6th:
[Field of Razors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1370), [Petrify](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628)
- 7th:
[Beheading Buzz Saw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1365), [Lifewood Cage](/Spells.aspx?ID=2006)
- 8th:
[Ferrous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1369), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644)
- 9th:
[Resplendent Mansion](/Spells.aspx?ID=2018)

**School Spells**
- Initial: [Augmented Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=2488)
- Advanced: [Conjured Clockwork](/Spells.aspx?ID=2487)
name: School of Magical Technologies

    right="Armor Specialization "
    pfs=""
>
[Chain](/ArmorGroups.aspx?ID=1)
</title>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 272

The armor is so flexible it can bend with a critical hit and absorb some of the blow. Reduce the damage from critical hits by either 4 + the value of the armor’s potency rune for medium armor, or 6 + the value of the armor’s potency rune for heavy armor. This can’t reduce the damage to less than the damage rolled for the hit before doubling for a critical hit.
name: Chain

Wood armor is generally flexible and light, but it can splinter as it breaks, throwing off shards and fragments that damage foes who deal you critical blows.
